program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_title: Right Now -- Rosângela Lands in Lisbon
lesson.menu_desc: Saying what is happening at this moment -- boarding, the flight, and the whole hotel front desk alone -- 38 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- Boarding and the Hotel
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 4 -- Boarding and the Hotel

listenings.text: Good afternoon, ladies and gentlemen. This is an announcement for the passengers of flight two nineteen to Lisbon. We are now boarding at gate twenty-two. We are starting with rows twenty to thirty, and after that we are calling all the other rows. Please have your passport and your boarding pass ready in your hand. When you are walking into the plane, a flight attendant is standing at the door, and she is helping you find your seat. Your carry-on goes in the overhead bin, above your seat, and your small bag goes under the seat in front of you. Please do not stand in the aisle, because other passengers are walking behind you. When everybody is sitting down, we are closing the door, so please close your seat belt and listen to the safety instructions. We are flying to Lisbon this afternoon, and the flight is nine hours and thirty minutes. We are landing in Lisbon tomorrow morning, at eight o'clock, local time. Thank you very much, and welcome on board.

Hello. My name is Sofia, and I am sitting here in the hotel lobby, like you. What a long day! Right now I am waiting for my husband. He is not here, because he is parking the car, and he is taking a long time: the street is very small and there are cars everywhere. And the receptionist? He is looking for our reservation on the computer right now. It is under my husband's name, not mine, and that is always a problem. You know, I usually travel with my sister, in September. But this year is different: right now I am traveling with my husband, in July, and I am looking forward to every day of it. Do you know what I am looking forward to the most? A hot shower and a big bed. We are staying here for six nights, and tomorrow we are visiting the old city. Oh, the receptionist is calling me now. He has our room key, and I think we are on the fourth floor. The elevator is over there, on the right. It was very nice to talk to you. Enjoy your stay!

paras: It is four o'clock in the afternoon, and Rosângela is standing at gate twenty-two. The flight to Lisbon is boarding right now. She is holding her passport and her boarding pass in her hand, and she is listening to the announcement. Her husband is standing next to her, and he is not saying anything, because today he does not need to. Rosângela is reading the screen herself.

Now she is walking into the plane. A flight attendant is standing at the door, and she is smiling. "Good afternoon. Eighteen C? It is on the right." Rosângela is putting her carry-on in the overhead bin, above her seat, and she is closing her seat belt. She usually sleeps on a night flight, but tonight she is not sleeping: she is looking out of the window, and she is thinking about tomorrow. Nine hours later, the plane is landing in Lisbon. It is eight o'clock in the morning, and the sun is shining.

At half past nine, they are arriving at the hotel. Rosângela is walking into the lobby with two bags, and she is going to the front desk alone. Her husband is sitting on a sofa, and he is waiting with the bags. "Good morning. We have a reservation, under the name Oliveira." The receptionist is looking at his computer, and he is not finding it. Rosângela is not panicking, and she is not looking for her husband. She is trying again, slowly: "Our reservation is under the name Pinheiro de Oliveira. We are staying for six nights." Now he is smiling. "Here it is. Room four eleven, on the fourth floor. Here is your room key, and the elevator is on your left." It is her first hour in Europe, and she is doing it alone.

gist.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Rosângela is sleeping on the plane, and her husband is talking to the receptionist for her.
Rosângela is arriving in Europe, and she is doing the whole first hour -- the gate, the plane and the hotel -- by herself.
There is a problem with the reservation, and Rosângela and her husband are looking for another hotel.

items: Rosângela is holding her passport and her boarding pass at the gate.
She is holding her passport and her boarding pass in her hand, and she is listening to the announcement.
Rosângela is sleeping on the flight to Lisbon.
She usually sleeps on a night flight, but tonight she is not sleeping. She is looking out of the window.
Rosângela is putting her carry-on in the overhead bin.
She is putting her carry-on in the overhead bin, above her seat.
The receptionist is finding the reservation under the name Oliveira.
He is not finding it under Oliveira. It is under her full name: Pinheiro de Oliveira.
Rosângela's husband is talking to the receptionist for her.
He is sitting on a sofa and he is waiting with the bags. She is going to the front desk alone.

items.situation: "Good afternoon. We are boarding rows twenty to thirty." Your seat is 18C. Say what is happening and what you are doing.
tips: That is my row. I am coming now.
I am looking for seat eighteen C.

items.situation: You are at the door of the plane and you cannot find your seat. Ask the flight attendant.
tips: Excuse me, where is seat eighteen C?
I am looking for my seat. Could you help me, please?

items.situation: Your carry-on is in your hand, and the overhead bin above your seat is full. Ask for help.
tips: Excuse me, the overhead bin is full. Where can I put my carry-on?
Could you help me, please? I am not finding a place for my bag.

items.situation: "Are you traveling alone today?" Answer, and say what your husband is doing right now.
tips: No, I am traveling with my husband.
He is sitting next to me. Right now he is sleeping.

items.situation: You are at the front desk of the hotel. Give your name and your reservation.
tips: Good morning. We have a reservation under the name Pinheiro de Oliveira.
We are staying for six nights.

items.situation: The receptionist is not finding your reservation. Do not panic: say it again, and help him.
tips: Could you look under Pinheiro de Oliveira, please? It is a long name.
I am sorry -- the reservation is under my full name, on my passport.

items.situation: You have your room key. Ask about breakfast, and ask where the elevator is.
tips: Excuse me, where is the elevator?

items.situation: The receptionist asks: "Is this your first time in Lisbon?" Tell him what you are looking forward to.
tips: I am looking forward to walking around the old city.
We are looking forward to a hot shower and a big bed!
